channelName: Pro Football Academy – The Next Gen of Ballers!

comments.author: @peterlaczai379
comments.text:
It's a very usefull practice video. Congrats. ✌️
Dominik's shooting techniques are his own unique ones. He developed them for himself after trainings from his very young ages and he didn't stoped still. Uncomperable with any shooting techniques in the world. When I was 12 I knew how to shoot knuckle ball too.✌️👍🫶
After a lot of practicing ofc.

comments.author: @محمد_2010-ه2ش
comments.text:
Finaly tutorial ❤❤❤
Thanksss

comments.author: @peterlaczai379
comments.text:
The video is good for learn the basic knuckle ball. Huge respect. 👏🫶
If we just look at the kicking technique, it is noticeable that Dominik shoots the ball differently. with brutal force, leaning a little on it, he puts his body weight in it, it barely spins, yet the curve of the ball is unpredictable for the first 15 meters, then it suddenly turns to the right. The goalkeeper moved in the opposite direction after the shot. then he just watched as the rocket hit with enormous power and a sudden change of direction. Unreal homing missle. 🚀

comments.text:
cool. anyway heres a brownie recipe
115 g (½ cup) butter
• 200 g (1 cup) sugar
• 2 eggs
• 1 tsp vanilla extract
• 40 g (⅓ cup) cocoa powder
• 65 g (½ cup) all-purpose flour
• ¼ tsp salt
• Optional: chocolate chips or nuts

Steps
1. Preheat oven to 175°C / 350°F.
Line or grease a baking pan.
2. Melt butter in a bowl (microwave or stovetop).
3. Mix in sugar, then add eggs one at a time.
Stir in vanilla.
4. Add cocoa powder, flour, and salt.
Mix just until combined (don’t overmix!).
5. Fold in extras if using.
6. Pour into pan and smooth the top.
7. Bake 20–25 minutes
A toothpick should come out with a few moist crumbs.
8. Cool, cut, and enjoy 😋

Tips
• Want fudgy brownies? Bake closer to 20 min.
• Want cakey brownies? Bake closer to 25–28 min.

comments.text:
Now I wanna tell my story:
I remember the first time I saw you, I was twelve, it was late winter, and until that day I only knew your name. You played Liverpool that year, unbeaten and heading for the title. But you stunned me: you fought every minute with fierce loyalty and stubborn heart, and in the end you won — leaving me breathless. Your name had already felt right in my chest before I even saw you, and after that match I fell so hard you became my Atleti, my team of love. We’ve celebrated and cried, won and lost, but never once gave up. You were always different, always better in spirit, and I’ve loved the way you never stopped fighting. Atleti, my champion. ❤️
You change my life too Atleti..

Here’s a simple homemade brownie recipe you can make easily 🍫
🍫 Easy Chocolate Brownies
📝 Ingredients:
1/2 cup (115g) butter
1 cup sugar
2 eggs
1 tsp vanilla extract
1/3 cup cocoa powder
1/2 cup flour
1/4 tsp salt
Optional: chocolate chips or nuts
👨‍🍳 Instructions:
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Melt the butter (microwave or stovetop).
Mix melted butter + sugar together.
Add eggs and vanilla. Mix well.
Add cocoa powder, flour, and salt. Mix until smooth (don’t overmix).
Pour into a greased baking pan.
Bake for 20–25 minutes.
Let cool before cutting (this makes them fudgier).
🔥 Tips:
For extra fudgy brownies → bake a little less time.
For cakey brownies → bake a little longer.
Add chocolate chips for extra gooey taste
